title: Subagent finally-cleanup contract for /tmp clones and run-dir worktrees
userRequest: Plan the implementation for subagent finally-cleanup contract for /tmp clones and run-dir worktrees
issueBody: Implementation subagents invoked via the Agent / Task tool inside a babysitter process sometimes need a working directory -- to clone the babysitter repo for a PR, to spin up a sandbox, etc. There is no current convention or SDK affordance for cleaning these up, which leads to leaked worktrees inside .a5c/runs/<runId>/work/ and no finally-style cleanup hook. A real incident left a 2.6 GB babysitter clone under .a5c/runs/.../work/babysitter, inflated vitest discovery from 442 to 1294 files, and produced confusing lint/typecheck failures. Proposed affordances: document that subagents needing a workspace MUST use /tmp/<descriptive-name>/ and never .a5c/runs/<runId>/; add pre-validation that find .a5c/runs -maxdepth 3 -name work -type d returns empty before JSON deliverables; preferably expose ProcessContext.onCleanup or a task cleanup callback that runs regardless of success, failure, or abort; or warn when a task returns with a non-empty .a5c/runs/<runId>/work directory.
triageComment: Root cause analysis: packages/sdk/src/runtime/types.ts exposes ProcessContext with runId, runDir, artifactsDir, task, breakpoint, subprocess, hook, etc., but no onCleanup/finally-style cleanup registration API. packages/sdk/src/runtime/processContext.ts constructs the context and artifactsDir but has no cleanup registry or lifecycle flush. packages/sdk/src/runtime/commitEffectResult.ts records task results and EFFECT_RESOLVED but does not invoke task cleanup callbacks and does not inspect <runDir>/work after task completion. packages/sdk/src/runtime/orchestrateIteration.ts calls run/iteration hooks, while packages/sdk/src/hooks/types.ts defines on-task-complete; the effect-resolution path does not currently appear to dispatch a task-complete cleanup/warning hook. plugins/babysitter-unified/skills/babysit/SKILL.md and .codex/skills/babysit/SKILL.md mention orchestrating .a5c/runs/<runId>/, but do not document the proposed /tmp-only convention for subagent scratch clones. Suggested approach: short-term docs guardrail, runtime guardrail warning/check after task result commit if <runDir>/work exists and is non-empty, and preferred SDK affordance ctx.onCleanup with tests around ProcessContext, commitEffectResult/iteration failure, and run completion/failure lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Babysit skill SKILL.md mentions the /tmp-only convention explicitly.
Preferred: ProcessContext exposes onCleanup so subagent workspace cleanup can be registered at process time and run in finally-style terminal paths.
Runtime warns when a task returns with a non-empty .a5c/runs/<runId>/work directory.
Cleanup behavior is covered by focused regression tests for success, failure/process-error, non-waiting semantics, and cleanup error handling.
Leak warning behavior is covered by focused tests for non-empty, empty, and absent <runDir>/work.
